full_prompt: Design the wing spar and primary wing structure of a human-powered glider (Human-powered Prop-plane division assumed) for a human-powered aircraft launch benchmark. The total wingspan must not exceed 36 m; this design targets a 25 m wingspan as representative. The aircraft launches from a 10 m high horizontal platform above the lake surface. The pilot is seated in a semi-reclined cockpit. The wing spar must carry a symmetric positive-g flight load of 2.5 g at an assumed gross weight of 120 kg (pilot 70 kg + aircraft 50 kg), giving a design limit load of 300 kg equivalent distributed span-wise in an elliptical lift distribution. The spar must also survive a negative-g load factor of -1.0 g for gusts. A mandatory pre-competition bend test requires the completed wing to support a root bending moment equivalent to the 2.5 g flight load held statically for at least 10 seconds with no audible cracking and no permanent deformation beyond 5 mm at the wingtip on unload. Material is typically a CFRP tube spar with balsa/foam ribs and heat-shrink film skin; the spar CFRP is modeled as linear elastic orthotropic with longitudinal E approximately 130 GPa (pultruded T300/epoxy). Primary verification is linear static structural FEA of the wing spar under the 2.5 g positive and 1.0 g negative load cases, with stress gated by CFRP laminate allowables. A linear buckling eigenvalue analysis is required on the upper skin / spar cap under the positive-g case with minimum load factor 1.5. Vibration (flutter) analysis is relevant in a certification context but is NOT required at this design gate. Thermal, fluid (sectional aerodynamic lift is already reduced to a distributed load on the spar), and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the wing structure, a mass budget, and a report listing total aircraft structural mass in kg, wingtip deflection at 2.5 g in mm, peak spar cap strain, and first buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Design the wing spar and primary wing structure of a human-powered glider (Human-powered Prop-plane division) for a human-powered aircraft launch benchmark. Wingspan 25 m (rulebook cap 36 m). Launch from a 10 m high horizontal platform above a lake. Gross weight 120 kg (70 kg pilot + 50 kg aircraft). Typical construction is a CFRP tube spar with balsa/foam ribs and heat-shrink film skin. Slender upper skin / spar cap must pass a linear buckling eigenvalue check with first-mode load factor >= 1.5 under the positive-g case.

Load cases.
- LC1 positive-g flight: 2.5 g symmetric with elliptical spanwise lift distribution (300 kg equivalent).
- LC2 negative-g gust: -1.0 g (-120 kg equivalent) with inverted elliptical distribution.
- LC3 mandatory pre-competition static bend test: root bending moment equivalent to LC1 held for 10 s; no audible cracking and residual wingtip deflection on unload <= 5 mm.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Elliptical lift distribution comes from lifting-line or panel-method aero, not FEA.

prompt.title: Human-powered glider 25 m wing spar
prompt.brief: Design a 25 m CFRP wing spar (of 36 m class) that carries 2.5 g flight load with elliptical lift, survives -1 g gust, passes static bend test.

load_cases.name: flight_2p5g_positive
load_cases.distribution: elliptical span-wise lift, 2.5 g at 120 kg gross

load_cases.name: static_bend_test
load_cases.description: root bending moment equivalent to LC1, held 10 s

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Flutter is relevant but not in this gate.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Lift reduced to distributed load via lifting-line.
